acceptance_targets: [CH08_CRAFT_01] By the end of the revised closing passage anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:225 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:261, a cold reader can separate evidence from inference: Vess's missing sister-name and cut-off anger, and Aldric's missing death/understanding are stated as facts, while the System's selection logic is either presented as a hypothesis or explicitly reconciled with both cases. Fail if the text still states, without qualification, that the System simply kept what was useful and stripped what was human.

[CH08_DIALOGUE_01] At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:131, the revised line contains no reference to entering "between the third and fourth rib" and no other near-verbatim echo of a style-bible example line. Pass only if Vess's replacement detail is newly specific, recognizably hers, and can stand beside the style-bible examples without sounding copied from them.

[OVERSTATEMENT_CH08] Pass if the gap concept is communicated through no more than two distinct formulations (one from Vess's voice, one from the door metaphor), with narrator summary at 2058 either removed or compressed to a single sentence that adds information rather than restating. Fail if the revised passage still cycles through three or more angles on the same absence-mechanism.

findings.acceptance_test: By the end of the revised closing passage anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:225 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:261, a cold reader can separate evidence from inference: Vess's missing sister-name and cut-off anger, and Aldric's missing death/understanding are stated as facts, while the System's selection logic is either presented as a hypothesis or explicitly reconciled with both cases. Fail if the text still states, without qualification, that the System simply kept what was useful and stripped what was human.
findings.problem: The closing movement settles too quickly on a neat theory that the System preserves utility and strips away the human. That reading fits parts of Vess's damage, but Aldric's missing material includes dangerous structural knowledge, not just feeling, so the chapter's interpretive frame outruns its own evidence. Because Maren restates the claim as fact in the ending, the scene explains the mystery into a tidy thesis instead of letting the more interesting uncertainty remain active.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the interpretive closures beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:225, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:231, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:239, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:261. Either distinguish multiple plausible logics for the edits on page (human attachment, incriminating knowledge, the circumstances of consumption) or keep Maren's utility-versus-human reading explicitly provisional. Preserve the state shift to responsibility, but anchor it to what this chapter has actually proven.

findings.acceptance_test: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:131, the revised line contains no reference to entering "between the third and fourth rib" and no other near-verbatim echo of a style-bible example line. Pass only if Vess's replacement detail is newly specific, recognizably hers, and can stand beside the style-bible examples without sounding copied from them.
findings.problem: Vess's retained-knowledge line copies a calibration-only style-bible image closely enough to register as blueprint leakage: the reveal hinges on her knowing how a blade goes in "between the third and fourth rib," which is a near-verbatim reuse of prohibited example-line material. Because it lands inside the chapter's defining proof of her incompleteness, it damages voice integrity on first read.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the borrowed operative image at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_08.md:131 with a fresh Vess-specific detail that proves the same point: she still retains professional kill-knowledge while personal memory has been excised. Keep the line concrete and trade-specific, but do not reuse the rib-entry construction or any other calibration phrasing from outline/style_bible.json.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the gap concept is communicated through no more than two distinct formulations (one from Vess's voice, one from the door metaphor), with narrator summary at 2058 either removed or compressed to a single sentence that adds information rather than restating. Fail if the revised passage still cycles through three or more angles on the same absence-mechanism.

evidence.excerpt: 2030: The pause stretched. Not the kind Vess controlled. Something worse: the silence of a mechanism reaching for a function that had been removed.

2048: *Angry. I know. I was angry. I can tell you that I was angry. The anger was — it was the reason I took the contract. Not the money. Not the guild's assignment protocols. This one was personal. The villages were — they were —*

2052: *The anger starts,* she said, and her voice was the same flat, measured voice it always was, and that was what made it horrible. *I can feel it begin. The beginning of the feeling is present. And then it stops. Not fades. Stops. The way a sound stops when you close a door. Except I didn't close it. The feeling was there, and then it was taken, and what remains is the knowledge that I had it. I can observe the room where the feeling was. I can see the door. I can't enter it.*

2058: Vess wasn't a tool. She wasn't a tactical readout with a voice. She was a woman who had taken a contract to kill a man who'd massacred fifty families, and she'd taken it for reasons she could no longer feel, and she had a sister whose name occupied a space in her memory shaped exactly like a name but containing nothing.

The space it occupied. I can tell you that I knew it better than any name I've ever known, better than Blackhall's, better than my own, and it isn't here.*

2056: Maren breathed. The contamination smell was in his mouth and in his throat and the hum was steady and the quest-light in the ditch glowed its soft, indifferent gold.

2058: Vess wasn't a tool. She wasn't a tactical readout with a voice. She was a woman who had taken a contract to kill a man who'd massacred fifty families, and she'd taken it for reasons she could no longer feel, and she had a sister whose name occupied a space in her memory shaped exactly like a name but containing nothing.
2059: 
2060: *I have them too,* Aldric said. Quiet. Formal. The voice of a man filing a report he didn't want to file. *The gaps.*

findings.problem: The memory-gap concept — what the System strips versus preserves in absorbed consciousnesses — cycles through four formulations that restate the same insight. Line 2030 describes the gap as 'the silence of a mechanism reaching for a function that had been removed.' Vess's detailed account of her preserved operational memory versus her stripped emotion (2036) is powerful. But then lines 2048-2052 run the same cycle again (anger starts, stops, the door metaphor), and line 2058 summarizes the entire concept as narrator commentary ('She was a woman who had taken a contract... for reasons she could no longer feel, and she had a sister whose name occupied a space in her memory shaped exactly like a name but containing nothing'). The door metaphor at 2052 is the strongest single formulation; the surrounding passes dilute rather than deepen it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2028-2060. Keep Vess's operational-memory passage (2036) and the door metaphor (2052) as the two anchor formulations. Compress or cut line 2030 (the 'mechanism' description overlaps with 2050's 'hit a wall'). At 2048-2051, compress the anger-starts-then-stops sequence: the sentence at 2050 ('The sentence didn't trail off. It hit a wall.') already communicates the abruptness — the preceding lines belabor the setup. Cut or compress the narrator summary at 2058 which re-explains Vess's entire situation in abstract terms that the scene has already rendered through her own voice.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel must commit fully to its own weirdness. A System-blind con man with a dead paladin and a dead assassin arguing in his skull is inherently absurd, and the novel should let that absurdity live alongside genuine grief, genuine violence, and genuine mystery without apologizing for the tonal range. The darkest moments and the funniest moments may occupy the same scene. When they do, let both registers operate simultaneously — do not sacrifice comedy for gravitas or gravitas for comedy. Risk means specificity: the specific sensation of a body doing things you did not tell it to do, the specific comedy of a dead paladin critiquing your hygiene while you're running for your life, the specific horror of carrying incomplete people whose memories have holes, the specific shame of learning that your eleven years of fraud may have killed people. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. The novel's emotional range should be wider than any single genre label would suggest.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in the narration of violence, bodily experience, moral compromise, or physical consequence is a draft-level failure. Maren is a con man who has seen hard things and done dirty work in poor villages for eleven years — his narration should not flinch where he would not flinch. The style bible's sensory bias toward tactile and proprioceptive detail means physical consequence must be rendered through the body: what it feels like to throw up, to kill someone, to have your muscles operate without your consent, to perform a ritual that requires you to open yourself to contamination. Summary and abstraction at the moment of consequence is always worse than presence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Aldric, whose low contraction level is a deliberate character marker that should erode over the course of the novel. Uncontracted forms in other characters' speech are reserved for emphasis, deliberation, or ceremony. In a world of practical people doing unglamorous work, stiff diction is the exception.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate between hot and quiet chapters as a structural principle. No two consecutive chapters should open the same way — if one opens with action, the next should open with interiority or setting; if one opens with dialogue, the next should open with physical sensation or observation. Vary scene count between chapters: compressed single-scene chapters after multi-scene ones. Let quiet chapters stay quiet — do not insert action beats or revelations to maintain pace. The pacing engine is Maren's voice and the interplay between the three internal consciousnesses, not external plot pressure.
prose_style_profile.diction: Colloquial with earned elevation. Default register is smart but not literary — a con man's English, precise about people and vague about feelings. Elevation reserved for moments of genuine awe, grief, or the rare glimpse of something beautiful. Fantasy-register portentousness is a draft-level failure — no 'ancient darkness' or 'powers beyond comprehension.' The world is weird and dangerous and ordinary all at once, and the prose should match.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Sentence fragments imitating action-movie or thriller prose — subject-dropping and article-stripping for false urgency
Semicolon-heavy scholarly narration that distances the reader from embodied experience
Present-tense micro-shifts for intensity — the novel is past tense throughout, no exceptions
Filtering words ('he saw,' 'he felt,' 'he noticed,' 'he realized') when direct rendering is available
Fantasy-voice portentousness: 'ancient,' 'dark power,' 'something stirred,' 'a presence beyond reckoning'
Comic narration that winks at the reader or breaks the fourth wall instead of committing to the situation
Repeated metaphor templates — if a comparison structure appears twice in a chapter, the second instance is a tic
Emotional summary replacing scene presence: 'He was overwhelmed by grief' instead of rendering what grief looks and feels like in the body
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Maren throughout. Aldric and Vess are experienced as internal voices — they speak as italicized dialogue within Maren's consciousness, clearly distinguished from his own thoughts. Maren cannot access their thoughts directly; he hears what they say to him, not what they think. The narration inherits Maren's sensory limitations: the System interface is always mediated through Aldric or Vess's descriptions, never rendered directly until the single exception in Chapter 17.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by emotional register. Default: medium-length sentences with periodic short declaratives for grounding. When Maren is performing (lying, charming, faking): smoother, more controlled rhythm with longer subordinate-clause chains. When Maren is honest or overwhelmed: choppier, more fragmented, more concrete. Fight scenes: visceral and slightly disoriented, reflecting Maren's System-blindness — sensation and proprioception without the visual data everyone else has. Quiet scenes: allow longer, more observational sentences. The rhythm should breathe differently in every chapter.
sensory_bias: tactile — Maren's primary sense channel; the physical world is how he navigates System-blindness
auditory — the hum of System activity, Aldric and Vess's voices, the specific sounds of quest-light and contamination
proprioceptive — Maren's body doing things he didn't choose; the internal sensation of carrying fragments; the physical cost of consumption
olfactory — contamination has smell; ritual has smell; the world stinks in specific ways that the System-sighted don't notice because they navigate by interface
